heading: Air quality criteria and control techniques
label: 7408 Air quality criteria and control techniques

children.heading: Air pollutant list; publication and revision by Administrator; issuance of air quality criteria for air pollutants
children.label: a Air pollutant list; publication and revision by Administrator; issuance of air quality criteria for air pollutants
A
children.text: emissions of which, in his judgment, cause or contribute to air pollution which may reasonably be anticipated to endanger public health or welfare;

children.label: B
children.text: the presence of which in the ambient air results from numerous or diverse mobile or stationary sources; and

children.label: C
children.text: for which air quality criteria had not been issued before but for which he plans to issue air quality criteria under this section.December 31, 1970

children.label: A
children.text: those variable factors (including atmospheric conditions) which of themselves or in combination with other factors may alter the effects on public health or welfare of such air pollutant;

children.label: B
children.text: the types of air pollutants which, when present in the atmosphere, may interact with such pollutant to produce an adverse effect on public health or welfare; and

children.label: C
children.text: any known or anticipated adverse effects on welfare.

children.heading: Issuance by Administrator of information on air pollution control techniques; standing consulting committees for air pollutants; establishment; membership
children.label: b Issuance by Administrator of information on air pollution control techniques; standing consulting committees for air pollutants; establishment; membership

children.text: Simultaneously with the issuance of criteria under subsection (a), the Administrator shall, after consultation with appropriate advisory committees and Federal departments and agencies, issue to the States and appropriate air pollution control agencies information on air pollution control techniques, which information shall include data relating to the cost of installation and operation, energy requirements, emission reduction benefits, and environmental impact of the emission control technology. Such information shall include such data as are available on available technology and alternative methods of prevention and control of air pollution. Such information shall also include data on alternative fuels, processes, and operating methods which will result in elimination or significant reduction of emissions.

In order to assist in the development of information on pollution control techniques, the Administrator may establish a standing consulting committee for each air pollutant included in a list published pursuant to subsection (a)(1), which shall be comprised of technically qualified individuals representative of State and local governments, industry, and the academic community. Each such committee shall submit, as appropriate, to the Administrator information related to that required by paragraph (1).

children.heading: Review, modification, and reissuance of criteria or information
children.label: c Review, modification, and reissuance of criteria or information
children.text: The Administrator shall from time to time review, and, as appropriate, modify, and reissue any criteria or information on control techniques issued pursuant to this section. Not later than six months after , the Administrator shall revise and reissue criteria relating to concentrations of NO over such period (not more than three hours) as he deems appropriate. Such criteria shall include a discussion of nitric and nitrous acids, nitrites, nitrates, nitrosamines, and other carcinogenic and potentially carcinogenic derivatives of oxides of nitrogen.August 7, 19772

children.heading: Publication in Federal Register; availability of copies for general public
children.label: d Publication in Federal Register; availability of copies for general public
children.text: The issuance of air quality criteria and information on air pollution control techniques shall be announced in the Federal Register and copies shall be made available to the general public.
methods to identify and evaluate alternative planning and control activities;
methods of reviewing plans on a regular basis as conditions change or new information is presented;
identification of funds and other resources necessary to implement the plan, including interagency agreements on providing such funds and resources;
methods to assure participation by the public in all phases of the planning process; and
such other methods as the Administrator determines necessary to carry out a continuous planning process.

children.heading: Information regarding processes, procedures, and methods to reduce or control pollutants in transportation; reduction of mobile source related pollutants; reduction of impact on public health
children.label: f Information regarding processes, procedures, and methods to reduce or control pollutants in transportation; reduction of mobile source related pollutants; reduction of impact on public health
i
children.text: programs for improved public transit;

children.label: ii
children.text: restriction of certain roads or lanes to, or construction of such roads or lanes for use by, passenger buses or high occupancy vehicles;

children.label: iii
children.text: employer-based transportation management plans, including incentives;

children.label: iv
children.text: trip-reduction ordinances;

children.label: v
children.text: traffic flow improvement programs that achieve emission reductions;

children.label: vi
children.text: fringe and transportation corridor parking facilities serving multiple occupancy vehicle programs or transit service;

children.label: vii
children.text: programs to limit or restrict vehicle use in downtown areas or other areas of emission concentration particularly during periods of peak use;

children.label: viii
children.text: programs for the provision of all forms of high-occupancy, shared-ride services;

children.label: ix
children.text: programs to limit portions of road surfaces or certain sections of the metropolitan area to the use of non-motorized vehicles or pedestrian use, both as to time and place;

children.label: x
children.text: programs for secure bicycle storage facilities and other facilities, including bicycle lanes, for the convenience and protection of bicyclists, in both public and private areas;

children.label: xi
children.text: programs to control extended idling of vehicles;

children.label: xii
children.text: programs to reduce motor vehicle emissions, consistent with subchapter II, which are caused by extreme cold start conditions;

children.label: xiii
children.text: employer-sponsored programs to permit flexible work schedules;

children.label: xiv
children.text: programs and ordinances to facilitate non-automobile travel, provision and utilization of mass transit, and to generally reduce the need for single-occupant vehicle travel, as part of transportation planning and development efforts of a locality, including programs and ordinances applicable to new shopping centers, special events, and other centers of vehicle activity;

children.label: xv
children.text: programs for new construction and major reconstructions of paths, tracks or areas solely for the use by pedestrian or other non-motorized means of transportation when economically feasible and in the public interest. For purposes of this clause, the Administrator shall also consult with the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: xvi
children.text: program to encourage the voluntary removal from use and the marketplace of pre-1980 model year light duty vehicles and pre-1980 model light duty trucks.2So in original. The period probably should be a semicolon.2

children.label: B
children.text: information on additional methods or strategies that will contribute to the reduction of mobile source related pollutants during periods in which any primary ambient air quality standard will be exceeded and during episodes for which an air pollution alert, warning, or emergency has been declared;

children.label: C
children.text: information on other measures which may be employed to reduce the impact on public health or protect the health of sensitive or susceptible individuals or groups; and

children.label: D
children.text: information on the extent to which any process, procedure, or method to reduce or control such air pollutant may cause an increase in the emissions or formation of any other pollutant.

children.label: A
children.text: the relative effectiveness of such processes, procedures, and methods;

children.label: B
children.text: the potential effect of such processes, procedures, and methods on transportation systems and the provision of transportation services; and

children.label: C
children.text: the environmental, energy, and economic impact of such processes, procedures, and methods.

children.heading: Assessment of risks to ecosystems
children.label: g Assessment of risks to ecosystems
children.text: The Administrator may assess the risks to ecosystems from exposure to criteria air pollutants (as identified by the Administrator in the Administrator’s sole discretion).

children.heading: RACT/BACT/LAER clearinghouse
children.label: h RACT/BACT/LAER clearinghouse
children.text: The Administrator shall make information regarding emission control technology available to the States and to the general public through a central database. Such information shall include all control technology information received pursuant to State plan provisions requiring permits for sources, including operating permits for existing sources.
